rawText:
China's decision to set its lowest growth target in a generation -- a cautious 4.5-5% range for 2026, announced at the National People's Congress in March -- reveals a government caught between the structural decline of an ageing, debt-laden economy and the political imperative to demonstrate that the Communist Party can still deliver prosperity.

The country's persistent deflation, which by some measures has lasted more than three years and has driven consumer prices to near-zero growth while factory-gate prices continue to fall, represents not merely a statistical curiosity but a symptom of chronic demand deficiency, suggesting that China's vast capital and labour remain dangerously underemployed.

Although the People's Bank of China could, in theory, cut its policy rate more aggressively now that a weakening yuan no longer poses the constraint it once did, the central bank finds itself shackled by another obstacle altogether -- the razor-thin profitability of Chinese lenders, whose margin between interest income and expenses has shrunk to a record low of 1.4%, far below the 1.8% regulators consider safe.

The government's reluctance to adopt a more ambitious fiscal stance, holding its headline budget deficit at 4% of GDP while refusing to expand local-government bond quotas, ensures that the grudging support on offer -- modest pension increases of 20 yuan a month and consumer-loan subsidies -- will prove woefully insufficient to close the gap between actual and potential output, which conservative estimates place at a level requiring growth above 5.3%.

Compounding these domestic vulnerabilities, the Iran war's disruption of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz has exposed China's dependence on energy imports, which account for roughly 13-14% of its total energy needs, and even the country's vast strategic reserves -- estimated at 120 days of import demand -- and its pricing formula that shields consumers from the worst spikes cannot prevent higher freight charges and input costs from rippling through supply chains.

A government campaign against "involution" -- the disorderly, self-defeating competition that plagues manufacturers, e-commerce firms, and even high-school exam-takers -- threatens to dampen the third engine of demand, investment, at precisely the moment when exports face headwinds from disrupted Middle Eastern shipping routes and when the scaled-back cash-for-clunkers programme can no longer deliver the consumer boost it provided in 2025.

The fight against air pollution, once a source of visible progress that helped legitimise party rule, has itself stalled in ways that mirror the broader growth dilemma: the easy gains from end-of-pipe measures like chimney scrubbers have been exhausted, and deeper structural changes -- electrifying transport, transforming industrial behaviour -- demand precisely the kind of bold, sustained investment the government seems unwilling to undertake.

China's leaders, haunted by the "flood-like" stimulus of 2007-09 that swept away financial discipline and carried inflation dangerously high, are allowing their instincts to remain rooted in the past even as deflation -- a fundamentally different threat -- erodes corporate revenues, depresses nominal growth, and increases the real burden of the country's enormous debts.

Nominal GDP growth, which makes no adjustment for inflation and therefore determines the actual revenue flowing into businesses and government coffers, has suffered so severely from falling prices that even meeting a diminished real growth target of 4.5% would leave the economy feeling far weaker than the headline figure suggests, perpetuating the vicious cycle of caution among consumers, investors, and policymakers alike.

What China needs, according to critics who invoke the old idiom about a traveller who carves a notch in a moving boat to mark where a sword fell overboard, is not the wait-and-see incrementalism of one or two rate cuts of 0.1 percentage points each, but rather the kind of comprehensive demand-side reform -- generous social spending, credible property-market backstops, and genuinely ambitious targets -- that would signal to a nation of anxious households that the government is willing to do whatever it takes to stop the deflationary drift before more water passes under the bridge.

overview.title: China's Growth Dilemma: Between Deflation and Ambition

sentences.original: China's decision to set its lowest growth target in a generation -- a cautious 4.5-5% range for 2026, announced at the National People's Congress in March -- reveals a government caught between the structural decline of an ageing, debt-laden economy and the political imperative to demonstrate that the Communist Party can still deliver prosperity.
sentences.translation: 중국이 한 세대 만에 가장 낮은 성장 목표치를 설정하기로 한 결정은, 즉 3월 전국인민대표대회에서 발표된 신중한 4.5~5% 범위인데, 고령화와 부채에 짓눌린 경제의 구조적 쇠퇴와 공산당이 여전히 번영을 가져다줄 수 있음을 보여줘야 하는 정치적 필요 사이에 끼인 정부의 모습을 드러낸다.

grammarElements.text: China's decision to set its lowest growth target
grammarElements.label: 주어 (소유격 + 명사 + to부정사)

grammarElements.text: reveals
grammarElements.label: 본동사

grammarElements.text: a government caught between ... and ...
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (과거분사 후치수식)

grammarElements.text: a cautious 4.5-5% range for 2026, announced at the National People's Congress in March
grammarElements.label: 삽입 동격어구

grammarElements.text: the political imperative to demonstrate that the Communist Party can still deliver prosperity
grammarElements.label: that절을 동반한 명사구

vocabulary.example: Economic stability is a political imperative for any ruling party.
The debt-laden company struggled to secure new investors.

expressions.meaning: A와 B 사이에 끼다, 진퇴양난에 빠지다
expressions.usage: 두 가지 상충하는 요구나 압력 사이에서 선택이 어려운 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Decision/Action] reveals [entity] caught between [force A] and [force B]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 결정이나 행위가 주체의 딜레마를 노출시킨다는 것을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The budget proposal reveals a ministry caught between austerity demands and voter expectations.
Her resignation reveals a CEO caught between shareholder pressure and ethical concerns.

drillQuestions.question: China's decision ... reveals a government ______ between the structural decline ... and the political imperative ...

sentences.notes: 문장 전체가 하나의 주절로 구성되어 있으며, 이중 대시로 삽입된 동격구가 구체적인 수치를 제공한다. caught between A and B 구문이 기사 전체의 핵심 갈등을 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: China's decision to set its lowest growth target in a generation
koreanAlignment.ko: 중국이 한 세대 만에 가장 낮은 성장 목표치를 설정하기로 한 결정

koreanAlignment.en: the structural decline of an ageing, debt-laden economy
koreanAlignment.ko: 고령화와 부채에 짓눌린 경제의 구조적 쇠퇴

koreanAlignment.en: the political imperative to demonstrate
can still deliver prosperity

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: framing statement (기사 전체의 핵심 갈등을 제시)

sentences.original: The country's persistent deflation, which by some measures has lasted more than three years and has driven consumer prices to near-zero growth while factory-gate prices continue to fall, represents not merely a statistical curiosity but a symptom of chronic demand deficiency, suggesting that China's vast capital and labour remain dangerously underemployed.
sentences.translation: 일부 지표로 보면 3년 이상 지속되어 소비자 물가를 거의 제로 성장으로 끌어내린 반면 공장도 가격은 계속 하락하고 있는 이 나라의 만성 디플레이션은 단순한 통계적 기이함이 아니라 만성적 수요 부족의 증상이며, 중국의 방대한 자본과 노동력이 위험할 정도로 충분히 활용되지 못하고 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: The country's persistent deflation
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which by some measures has lasted more than three years and has driven consumer prices to near-zero growth
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (주어 보충 설명)

grammarElements.text: represents
grammarElements.label: 본동사

grammarElements.text: not merely A but B
grammarElements.label: 상관접속사 구문

grammarElements.text: suggesting that China's vast capital and labour remain dangerously underemployed
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (부가 설명)

vocabulary.example: Persistent deflation discourages spending because consumers expect lower prices tomorrow.
Many graduates remain underemployed in jobs that do not require their skills.

expressions.usage: 단순한 해석을 넘어 더 깊은 의미를 전달하고자 할 때 사용하는 강조 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] represents not merely [surface reading] but [deeper meaning], suggesting that ...
patterns.explanation: 현상의 피상적 해석을 부정하고 더 깊은 의미를 제시한 뒤, 그것이 암시하는 바를 덧붙이는 패턴
examples: The drop in enrolment represents not merely a demographic shift but a crisis of confidence in higher education.
The protest represents not merely anger at prices but a symptom of deeper social inequality.

drillQuestions.question: The country's persistent deflation represents not merely a statistical curiosity but a ______ of chronic demand deficiency.

sentences.notes: not merely A but B 구문과 suggesting 분사구문의 결합으로, 디플레이션의 표면적 현상 뒤에 숨은 구조적 문제를 단계적으로 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: The country's persistent deflation
not merely a statistical curiosity
a symptom of chronic demand deficiency
remain dangerously underemployed
koreanAlignment.ko: 위험할 정도로 충분히 활용되지 못하고 있다

pronunciationNotes: deflation: /dɪˈfleɪʃən/ — de에 약한 강세, fla에 주강세
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: litotes (not merely ... but — 이중 부정을 통한 강조)

sentences.original: Although the People's Bank of China could, in theory, cut its policy rate more aggressively now that a weakening yuan no longer poses the constraint it once did, the central bank finds itself shackled by another obstacle altogether -- the razor-thin profitability of Chinese lenders, whose margin between interest income and expenses has shrunk to a record low of 1.4%, far below the 1.8% regulators consider safe.
sentences.translation: 이론적으로 중국인민은행은 위안화 약세가 더 이상 과거와 같은 제약을 가하지 않는 지금 기준금리를 더 공격적으로 인하할 수 있겠지만, 중앙은행은 전혀 다른 장애물에 묶여 있다. 바로 중국 금융기관들의 극도로 얇은 수익성인데, 이자수익과 비용 사이의 마진이 규제당국이 안전하다고 보는 1.8%에 한참 못 미치는 사상 최저 수준인 1.4%로 줄어들었다.

grammarElements.text: Although the People's Bank of China could ... cut its policy rate more aggressively
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (Although)

grammarElements.text: now that a weakening yuan no longer poses the constraint it once did
grammarElements.label: now that 원인절

grammarElements.text: the central bank finds itself shackled by another obstacle altogether
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: the razor-thin profitability of Chinese lenders
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구

vocabulary.example: Banks find themselves shackled by regulations that limit their ability to lend.

expressions.usage: 이익률, 다수결 차이 등이 극히 작은 상황을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 이론적 가능성이 있지만 실제로는 다른 제약에 묶여 있음을 대비시키는 패턴
examples: Although the Fed could raise rates further, it finds itself constrained by fears of recession.
Although the company could expand overseas, it finds itself hampered by regulatory hurdles.

drillQuestions.question: The central bank finds itself ______ by another obstacle altogether.

sentences.notes: 양보절(Although)과 주절의 대비가 핵심이다. 환율 제약이 사라졌지만 은행 수익성이라는 새로운 장애물이 등장했음을 보여준다. shackled라는 은유적 표현이 중앙은행의 무력감을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Although the People's Bank of China could cut its policy rate more aggressively
koreanAlignment.ko: 중국인민은행은 기준금리를 더 공격적으로 인하할 수 있겠지만

koreanAlignment.en: now that a weakening yuan no longer poses the constraint it once did
koreanAlignment.ko: 위안화 약세가 더 이상 과거와 같은 제약을 가하지 않는 지금

koreanAlignment.en: the central bank finds itself shackled by another obstacle altogether
koreanAlignment.ko: 중앙은행은 전혀 다른 장애물에 묶여 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the razor-thin profitability of Chinese lenders

pronunciationNotes: shackled: /ˈʃækəld/ — ck 뒤에 le가 올 때 /əld/로 부드럽게 연결

sentences.original: The government's reluctance to adopt a more ambitious fiscal stance, holding its headline budget deficit at 4% of GDP while refusing to expand local-government bond quotas, ensures that the grudging support on offer -- modest pension increases of 20 yuan a month and consumer-loan subsidies -- will prove woefully insufficient to close the gap between actual and potential output, which conservative estimates place at a level requiring growth above 5.3%.
sentences.translation: 정부가 더 과감한 재정 기조를 채택하기를 꺼리며 총 재정적자를 GDP의 4%에 묶어두고 지방정부 채권 한도 확대를 거부하는 것은, 마지못해 내놓은 지원책인 월 20위안의 소폭 연금 인상과 소비자 대출 보조금이 실제 산출과 잠재 산출 사이의 격차를 메우기에 한탄스러울 정도로 부족할 것임을 보장하는데, 보수적 추산에 따르면 그 격차를 메우려면 5.3% 이상의 성장이 필요하다.

grammarElements.text: The government's reluctance to adopt a more ambitious fiscal stance
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: holding ... while refusing ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (동시 동작)

grammarElements.text: ensures that
grammarElements.label: 본동사 + that절

grammarElements.text: the grudging support on offer
grammarElements.label: that절 내 주어

grammarElements.text: will prove woefully insufficient
grammarElements.label: that절 내 서술어

vocabulary.example: The apology was grudging and failed to satisfy the critics.
The response was woefully inadequate given the scale of the crisis.

expressions.phrase: close the gap between A and B
expressions.usage: 수치, 실력, 기대치 등의 차이를 좁힌다는 맥락에서 사용

patterns.explanation: 주체의 소극적 태도가 부정적 결과를 필연적으로 만들어낸다는 인과 패턴
examples: The board's reluctance to invest in R&D ensures that the company will fall behind its competitors.
Their reluctance to negotiate ensures that the conflict will drag on.

drillQuestions.question: The government's reluctance to adopt a more ambitious fiscal stance ensures that the grudging support on offer will prove ______ insufficient.

sentences.notes: reluctance → ensures → woefully insufficient의 논리 흐름이 정부의 소극적 태도와 그 필연적 실패를 연결한다. grudging이라는 형용사 하나로 정부의 마지못한 태도를 효과적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The government's reluctance to adopt a more ambitious fiscal stance
koreanAlignment.ko: 정부가 더 과감한 재정 기조를 채택하기를 꺼리는 것

koreanAlignment.en: the grudging support on offer
will prove woefully insufficient
the gap between actual and potential output
koreanAlignment.ko: 실제 산출과 잠재 산출 사이의 격차

sentences.original: Compounding these domestic vulnerabilities, the Iran war's disruption of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz has exposed China's dependence on energy imports, which account for roughly 13-14% of its total energy needs, and even the country's vast strategic reserves -- estimated at 120 days of import demand -- and its pricing formula that shields consumers from the worst spikes cannot prevent higher freight charges and input costs from rippling through supply chains.
sentences.translation: 이러한 국내 취약성에 더해, 이란 전쟁으로 호르무즈 해협을 통한 석유 수송이 중단되면서 총 에너지 수요의 약 13~14%를 차지하는 중국의 에너지 수입 의존도가 드러났으며, 수입량 120일분으로 추산되는 방대한 전략 비축유와 소비자를 최악의 급등으로부터 보호하는 가격 공식조차도 운임 인상과 투입 비용이 공급망 전체에 파급되는 것을 막지 못한다.

grammarElements.text: Compounding these domestic vulnerabilities
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (부가 상황)

grammarElements.text: the Iran war's disruption of oil shipments
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has exposed
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: even the country's vast strategic reserves ... cannot prevent
grammarElements.label: even 강조 + 주절

vocabulary.example: Supply disruptions are compounding the economic damage from sanctions.

expressions.usage: 어떤 충격이나 영향이 시스템 전체로 퍼져나갈 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.explanation: 기존 문제에 더해 새로운 사태가 또 다른 약점을 드러낸다는 복합 악재 패턴
examples: Compounding the housing crisis, rising interest rates have exposed the fragility of household finances.
Compounding supply shortages, a port strike has exposed the country's logistical weaknesses.

drillQuestions.question: ______ these domestic vulnerabilities, the Iran war's disruption has exposed China's dependence on energy imports.

sentences.notes: 분사구문(Compounding)으로 시작해 국내 취약성 위에 대외 충격이 겹치는 구조를 보여준다. even ... cannot prevent 구문이 비축유마저 충분하지 않다는 점을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Compounding these domestic vulnerabilities
the Iran war's disruption of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란 전쟁으로 호르무즈 해협을 통한 석유 수송이 중단된 것

koreanAlignment.en: has exposed China's dependence on energy imports
cannot prevent higher freight charges and input costs from rippling through supply chains
koreanAlignment.ko: 운임 인상과 투입 비용이 공급망 전체에 파급되는 것을 막지 못한다

sentences.original: A government campaign against "involution" -- the disorderly, self-defeating competition that plagues manufacturers, e-commerce firms, and even high-school exam-takers -- threatens to dampen the third engine of demand, investment, at precisely the moment when exports face headwinds from disrupted Middle Eastern shipping routes and when the scaled-back cash-for-clunkers programme can no longer deliver the consumer boost it provided in 2025.
sentences.translation: 제조업체, 전자상거래 기업, 심지어 수험생까지 괴롭히는 무질서하고 자멸적인 경쟁인 '내권(인볼루션)'에 대한 정부의 단속은, 수출이 중동 해상 항로 중단이라는 역풍에 직면하고 축소된 노후차 교환 프로그램이 2025년에 제공했던 소비 부양을 더 이상 기대할 수 없는 바로 그 시점에, 수요의 세 번째 엔진인 투자를 위축시킬 우려가 있다.

grammarElements.text: A government campaign against "involution"
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: the disorderly, self-defeating competition that plagues ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구

grammarElements.text: threatens to dampen
grammarElements.label: 본동사 (threaten to)

grammarElements.text: the third engine of demand, investment
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 동격

grammarElements.text: at precisely the moment when ... and when ...
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 (이중 when절)

vocabulary.example: China's tech sector suffers from involution, where companies copy each other into unprofitability.

expressions.usage: 경제나 사업이 외부 요인으로 인해 어려움에 처할 때 사용하는 비유적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Action] threatens to [negative outcome] at precisely the moment when [bad timing]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 조치가 최악의 타이밍에 부정적 결과를 초래할 위험이 있음을 강조하는 패턴
examples: The new tax threatens to discourage investment at precisely the moment when the economy needs a boost.
The regulation threatens to stifle innovation at precisely the moment when competition is intensifying.

drillQuestions.question: A government campaign against involution threatens to ______ the third engine of demand.

sentences.notes: at precisely the moment when 구문이 정책의 타이밍 문제를 날카롭게 지적한다. involution은 중국 사회에서 유행하는 개념으로, 과잉 경쟁의 자멸적 특성을 함축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A government campaign against "involution"
the disorderly, self-defeating competition
threatens to dampen the third engine of demand, investment
koreanAlignment.ko: 수요의 세 번째 엔진인 투자를 위축시킬 우려가 있다

koreanAlignment.en: at precisely the moment when exports face headwinds
koreanAlignment.ko: 수출이 역풍에 직면한 바로 그 시점에

sentences.original: The fight against air pollution, once a source of visible progress that helped legitimise party rule, has itself stalled in ways that mirror the broader growth dilemma: the easy gains from end-of-pipe measures like chimney scrubbers have been exhausted, and deeper structural changes -- electrifying transport, transforming industrial behaviour -- demand precisely the kind of bold, sustained investment the government seems unwilling to undertake.
sentences.translation: 한때 당의 통치를 정당화하는 데 기여한 가시적 성과의 원천이었던 대기오염과의 싸움은 그 자체가 더 넓은 성장 딜레마를 반영하는 방식으로 교착 상태에 빠졌다. 굴뚝 세정기 같은 말단 처리 조치를 통한 쉬운 성과는 이미 소진되었고, 교통의 전기화와 산업 행태의 전환이라는 더 깊은 구조적 변화는 정부가 실행할 의지가 없어 보이는 바로 그런 과감하고 지속적인 투자를 요구한다.

grammarElements.text: The fight against air pollution
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: once a source of visible progress that helped legitimise party rule
grammarElements.label: 삽입 동격 (과거 상황)

grammarElements.text: has itself stalled
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사 (재귀 강조)

grammarElements.text: in ways that mirror the broader growth dilemma
grammarElements.label: 방법 부사구

vocabulary.example: Economic growth has long been used to legitimise single-party rule.

expressions.meaning: 말단 처리 조치, 사후 대응 방식
expressions.usage: 오염이 발생한 후 배출구에서 처리하는 방식을 가리키며, 근본적 해결과 대비되는 개념

patterns.pattern: [Achievement], once [past success], has itself stalled in ways that mirror [broader problem]
patterns.explanation: 과거에 성공적이었던 것이 더 큰 문제를 반영하며 정체된 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: Education reform, once a source of national pride, has itself stalled in ways that mirror the broader inequality crisis.
Diplomatic engagement, once a pillar of foreign policy, has itself stalled in ways that mirror domestic political gridlock.

drillQuestions.question: The fight against air pollution has itself ______ in ways that mirror the broader growth dilemma.

sentences.notes: mirror라는 동사가 대기오염 문제와 경제 성장 딜레마 사이의 구조적 유사성을 효과적으로 연결한다. 콜론(:) 뒤에서 구체적 설명이 이어지는 전형적인 저널리즘 문장 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The fight against air pollution
once a source of visible progress that helped legitimise party rule
koreanAlignment.ko: 한때 당의 통치를 정당화하는 데 기여한 가시적 성과의 원천

koreanAlignment.en: has itself stalled
the kind of bold, sustained investment the government seems unwilling to undertake
koreanAlignment.ko: 정부가 실행할 의지가 없어 보이는 과감하고 지속적인 투자

pronunciationNotes: legitimise: /lɪˈdʒɪtɪmaɪz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세, 영국식 철자

sentences.original: China's leaders, haunted by the "flood-like" stimulus of 2007-09 that swept away financial discipline and carried inflation dangerously high, are allowing their instincts to remain rooted in the past even as deflation -- a fundamentally different threat -- erodes corporate revenues, depresses nominal growth, and increases the real burden of the country's enormous debts.
sentences.translation: 금융 기강을 휩쓸어 버리고 인플레이션을 위험한 수준까지 끌어올린 2007~09년의 '홍수 같은' 부양책에 사로잡힌 중국 지도부는, 근본적으로 다른 위협인 디플레이션이 기업 수익을 잠식하고 명목 성장을 억누르며 거대한 부채의 실질 부담을 늘리고 있는 상황에서도 자신들의 본능이 과거에 머물러 있도록 내버려 두고 있다.

grammarElements.text: China's leaders
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: haunted by the "flood-like" stimulus of 2007-09
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 후치수식

grammarElements.text: are allowing their instincts to remain rooted in the past
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 동사구 (allow + 목적격보어)

grammarElements.text: even as deflation erodes ... depresses ... and increases ...
grammarElements.label: even as 양보절 (삼중 동사)

vocabulary.example: The CEO remained haunted by the failed merger that nearly bankrupted the firm.

expressions.usage: 사고방식이나 태도가 특정 시기나 관점에 고착되어 있을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Leaders], haunted by [past trauma], are [present mistake] even as [current threat]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 트라우마가 현재의 잘못된 대응을 낳고 있음을 대비시키는 패턴
examples: European leaders, haunted by the inflation of the 1970s, are tightening policy even as recession looms.
Investors, haunted by the dot-com crash, are avoiding tech stocks even as the sector surges.

drillQuestions.question: China's leaders, ______ by the "flood-like" stimulus of 2007-09, are allowing their instincts to remain rooted in the past.

sentences.notes: haunted라는 단어가 과거 정책 실패가 현재 지도부의 심리에 미치는 영향을 생생하게 전달한다. even as절의 세 동사(erodes, depresses, increases)가 디플레이션의 삼중 파괴력을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: China's leaders, haunted by the "flood-like" stimulus of 2007-09
are allowing their instincts to remain rooted in the past
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신들의 본능이 과거에 머물러 있도록 내버려 두고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: even as deflation erodes corporate revenues
increases the real burden of the country's enormous debts

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: psychological metaphor (과거 트라우마에 의한 정책 마비)

sentences.original: Nominal GDP growth, which makes no adjustment for inflation and therefore determines the actual revenue flowing into businesses and government coffers, has suffered so severely from falling prices that even meeting a diminished real growth target of 4.5% would leave the economy feeling far weaker than the headline figure suggests, perpetuating the vicious cycle of caution among consumers, investors, and policymakers alike.
sentences.translation: 인플레이션을 조정하지 않아 기업과 정부 금고에 실제로 유입되는 수입을 결정하는 명목 GDP 성장률은 물가 하락으로 너무 심하게 타격을 받아, 축소된 실질 성장 목표인 4.5%를 달성하더라도 경제는 대외 발표 수치가 암시하는 것보다 훨씬 약하게 느껴질 것이며, 소비자와 투자자, 정책 입안자 모두의 신중함이라는 악순환을 영속시킬 것이다.

grammarElements.text: Nominal GDP growth
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which makes no adjustment for inflation and therefore determines ...
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

grammarElements.text: has suffered so severely ... that
grammarElements.label: so ... that 결과 구문

grammarElements.text: perpetuating the vicious cycle
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Tax revenue flowing into government coffers has been declining.
Austerity risks perpetuating the very recession it aims to solve.

expressions.usage: 한 문제가 다른 문제를 낳고 그것이 다시 원래 문제를 악화시키는 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Metric] has suffered so severely from [cause] that even [modest achievement] would leave [situation worse than expected]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 지표가 심각하게 악화되어 최소한의 목표 달성으로도 상황이 나아 보이지 않음을 강조하는 패턴
examples: Consumer confidence has suffered so severely from layoffs that even a recovery in hiring would leave sentiment depressed.
Revenue has suffered so severely from the downturn that even a modest rebound would leave the company short of its targets.

drillQuestions.question: Nominal GDP growth has suffered so severely from falling prices that even meeting a diminished real growth target would leave the economy feeling far weaker, ______ the vicious cycle of caution.

sentences.notes: so severely ... that 구문이 명목 GDP와 실질 GDP의 괴리가 얼마나 심각한지를 드러낸다. 마지막 분사구문(perpetuating)이 이 악순환의 자기강화적 성격을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Nominal GDP growth
the actual revenue flowing into businesses and government coffers
koreanAlignment.ko: 기업과 정부 금고에 실제로 유입되는 수입

koreanAlignment.en: has suffered so severely from falling prices
koreanAlignment.ko: 물가 하락으로 너무 심하게 타격을 받아

koreanAlignment.en: perpetuating the vicious cycle of caution

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: paradox (목표 달성이 오히려 약함을 드러내는 역설)

sentences.original: What China needs, according to critics who invoke the old idiom about a traveller who carves a notch in a moving boat to mark where a sword fell overboard, is not the wait-and-see incrementalism of one or two rate cuts of 0.1 percentage points each, but rather the kind of comprehensive demand-side reform -- generous social spending, credible property-market backstops, and genuinely ambitious targets -- that would signal to a nation of anxious households that the government is willing to do whatever it takes to stop the deflationary drift before more water passes under the bridge.
sentences.translation: 움직이는 배에 칼자국을 새겨 칼이 물에 빠진 위치를 표시한다는 옛 고사를 인용하는 비평가들에 따르면, 중국에 필요한 것은 0.1%포인트씩 한두 번 금리를 인하하는 관망형 점진주의가 아니라, 넉넉한 사회적 지출과 신뢰할 수 있는 부동산 시장 안전장치, 그리고 진정으로 야심찬 목표를 포함하는 포괄적 수요 측면의 개혁, 즉 더 많은 물이 다리 밑으로 흐르기 전에 디플레이션의 표류를 멈추기 위해 정부가 무엇이든 할 의지가 있다는 것을 불안한 가정들로 이루어진 국가에 보여줄 그런 개혁이다.

grammarElements.text: What China needs
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사 what절 (주어)

grammarElements.text: according to critics who invoke the old idiom
grammarElements.label: 삽입 부사구

grammarElements.text: is not A but rather B
grammarElements.label: not A but B 대비 구조

grammarElements.text: the kind of comprehensive demand-side reform ... that would signal
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 보어

grammarElements.text: whatever it takes
grammarElements.label: 양보 관계절

vocabulary.example: Critics argue that incrementalism is no match for a crisis of this scale.
The central bank promised to act as a backstop for the financial system.

expressions.usage: 결연한 의지를 강조할 때 쓰는 표현으로, 마리오 드라기 ECB 총재의 유로 위기 발언으로 유명해짐

patterns.pattern: What [entity] needs is not [insufficient measure] but rather [comprehensive solution] that would [desired effect]
patterns.explanation: 부족한 대응을 비판하고 필요한 포괄적 해결책을 제시하는 수사적 패턴
examples: What the NHS needs is not another round of budget cuts but rather a comprehensive staffing strategy that would restore patient confidence.
What the industry needs is not piecemeal regulation but rather a coherent framework that would level the playing field.

drillQuestions.question: What China needs is not the wait-and-see ______ of one or two rate cuts, but rather comprehensive demand-side reform.

sentences.notes: 각주선(刻舟求劍)이라는 중국 고사성어가 영어로 풀어서 설명되어 있다. 배는 움직이는데 칼자국에만 매달리는 어리석음을 비유해 정부의 점진주의를 비판한다. whatever it takes는 드라기의 유명한 ECB 발언을 상기시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: What China needs
the wait-and-see incrementalism
comprehensive demand-side reform
whatever it takes to stop the deflationary drift
koreanAlignment.ko: 디플레이션의 표류를 멈추기 위해 무엇이든 할 것

koreanAlignment.en: before more water passes under the bridge
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 많은 물이 다리 밑으로 흐르기 전에

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: classical allusion + call to action (고사성어 인용과 행동 촉구)
